q: What is mutualism, and which of the following is a correctly named example from the IB syllabus?
options: A relationship where one species benefits and the other is unaffected; e.g., barnacles on whale skin
A relationship where both species benefit; e.g., mycorrhizal fungi associated with orchid roots
A relationship where one species benefits at the expense of the other; e.g., Rhizobium bacteria in legume root nodules
A competitive relationship where both species suffer reduced fitness; e.g., zooxanthellae expelled from coral during bleaching
explanation: Mutualism benefits both partners; mycorrhizae in orchids (fungi provide minerals, plant provides carbohydrates) is one of three named IB examples alongside root nodules in legumes and zooxanthellae in hard corals.
